text: Large language models, or LLMs, are a type of artificial intelligence trained on massive amounts of text from the internet, books, and articles. They work by predicting the next most likely word in a sequence based on patterns in their training data. Users interact with them through prompts—specific instructions or questions. The art of crafting effective prompts is known as prompt engineering. LLMs can generate essays, answer questions, and even write code. However, they have limits: they may produce incorrect or biased information, lack true understanding, and cannot learn new facts from a single conversation.
